# Dev Plan: CLI Agent Resilience & Discoverability

**Source:** `smoke-test.md` (2026-05-11)
**Generated by:** `/dev-planner`
**Status:** Ready for issue creation

---

## Background

The smoke test against running `Rhythm.app` (pid 26359) found:

- Installed app bundle is stale relative to repo source — `/agent-configs`, config-driven `/agents/capabilities`, and the `agent_configs` migration are not present in the bundled `api_server/dist`.
- Local agent server was not listening on `:4001` during the test; `/health`, `/agents/capabilities`, `/agent-configs`, `/agent-sessions` all refused connections.
- The selected Codex session in the live UI stayed pinned at `Starting` / `Session started. Waiting for output...` even though the DB had marked recent sessions as `closed`.
- `New agent session` dialog only exposes `Codex` (hardcoded branches in `agents_view.dart`); even after rebuild, the dialog won't dynamically render configured agents.
- `codex` and `claude` resolve from a login shell, so this is not a missing-CLI problem.

A `Manage agents` link **already exists in source** at `agents_view.dart:431` next to the `+ New` button. It only appears missing in the live app because the bundle is stale; the rebuild step at the end resolves it. No new entry-point work is needed.

## Scope

Four areas of work, plus a final manual rebuild step:

1. **Stale `Starting` session detection + recovery** — auto-detect when the backend session is gone (server-health poll + WS reconnect-failure timer); add a single "Reconnect" button and a client-side "Cancel" fallback that works when the backend is unreachable.
2. **New Session dialog: dynamic agent list** — render from `AgentConfigsController.enabledAgents`; show disabled "(not installed)" entries for configured agents whose CLI is missing.
3. **Agent server health visibility + manual restart** — small status dot in the Agents header, inline banner with a "Restart agent server" button inside the Agents view when status ≠ ready, periodic `/health` poll every 15s paused when the app is backgrounded, two-consecutive-failure guard against flapping.
4. **Soft "stuck-Starting" hint** — when a session sits in `starting` for >30s with empty output buffer, show a subtle warning in the row.
5. **Manual finalization** — rebuild + reinstall app so the bundled `api_server` has `/agent-configs`, config-driven capabilities, and the `agent_configs` migration.

## Design decisions

- **No new `degraded` state on `AgentServerStatus`.** `ready ↔ failed` is sufficient; a new failure reason (`lostConnection`) distinguishes the UX message without bloating switches everywhere.
- **`HealthPoller` is a new reusable utility** (`lib/app/core/agents/health_poller.dart`) with injected `checkFn`, lifecycle-aware pause, transition-only callbacks, two-consecutive-failure guard.
- **`AgentSessionConnectivity` is a new value type** on `AgentsController` (typed `isWsDisconnected` + `stuckSessionIds`), surfaced as a single `connectivity` getter.
- **`connectivityStream` lives in `AgentsDataSource`** (closest to the WS connection); `AgentsController` subscribes and exposes derived state to the view.
- **UI widgets stay as private `_Foo` widgets in `agents_view.dart`** (matches existing convention: `_StatusDot`, `_SessionRow`, etc. are all private widgets in that file). Extraction to separate files is busywork.
- **Tests required for controller / data-source / repository changes**, not for pure widget rendering. Manual smoke test at the end covers UI.

## Milestones (4 phases)

| Phase | Name | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| 1 | Foundation | `HealthPoller` utility, `lostConnection` failure reason, server health polling, WS connectivity stream |
| 2 | Controller logic | `AgentSessionConnectivity` model, stuck-session tracking, `reconnectSession`, offline `closeSession` fallback |
| 3 | UI | Status dot, inline banner, Reconnect button, stuck hint, dynamic agent list in New Session dialog |
| 4 | Manual finalization | Rebuild + reinstall app; verify with smoke checks |

## Issues (in implementation order)

> Each issue body uses the repo's `ai-coding-task.md` template: Goal · Files to touch · Requirements · Constraints · Acceptance criteria · Tests to update.

### Phase 1: Foundation

---

#### Issue 1 — Add `HealthPoller` utility with lifecycle-aware pause

**Title:** `Add HealthPoller utility class`
**Milestone:** Phase 1: Foundation
**Labels:** (use any existing `enhancement` / `cli-agents` / `infrastructure` labels)

##### Goal

Add a reusable utility class that periodically calls an async health-check function, pauses while the app is backgrounded, fires a callback only on health-state transitions, and tolerates transient flaps via a 2-consecutive-failure guard.

##### Files to touch

- CREATE `apps/desktop_flutter/lib/app/core/agents/health_poller.dart`
- CREATE `apps/desktop_flutter/test/app/core/agents/health_poller_test.dart`

##### Requirements

Public surface:

```dart
class HealthPoller with WidgetsBindingObserver {
  HealthPoller({
    required Future<bool> Function() checkFn,
    required void Function(bool isHealthy) onHealthChanged,
    Duration interval = const Duration(seconds: 15),
    int failureThreshold = 2,
  });

  void start();
  void dispose();
}
```

Behavior:

- On `start()`: register as `WidgetsBindingObserver`, run a check immediately, then `Timer.periodic(interval, ...)`.
- Each tick: call `checkFn()`. If it returns `true`, reset the consecutive-failure counter. If it returns `false`, increment the counter; once the counter reaches `failureThreshold`, transition health from `true` → `false` and fire `onHealthChanged(false)`. On the first `true` after a `false` state, fire `onHealthChanged(true)`.
- `onHealthChanged` fires only on transitions, not every tick.
- On `didChangeAppLifecycleState(AppLifecycleState != resumed)`: cancel the timer.
- On `resumed`: run a check immediately, then restart the periodic timer.
- On `dispose()`: cancel the timer and remove the observer. Idempotent.

##### Constraints

- Pure Dart + `package:flutter/widgets.dart` for `WidgetsBindingObserver`. No `http` or `dart:io` here — `checkFn` is injected.
- Do not depend on any controller. This is a leaf utility.

##### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Class compiles; `dart format` clean; `flutter analyze --no-fatal-infos` clean.
- [ ] Unit tests cover: state-change callback fires only on transition; 2 consecutive failures required before signalling false; lifecycle pause stops timer; lifecycle resume restarts timer with an immediate check; `dispose()` is idempotent.

##### Tests to update

- New file: `apps/desktop_flutter/test/app/core/agents/health_poller_test.dart`

---

#### Issue 2 — Add `lostConnection` to `AgentServerFailureReason`

**Title:** `Add lostConnection failure reason for agent server`
**Milestone:** Phase 1: Foundation
**Depends on:** —

##### Goal

Distinguish "the agent server stopped responding after it was once healthy" from the existing startup failure reasons, so the UI can show an actionable message.

##### Files to touch

- `apps/desktop_flutter/lib/app/core/server/api_server_service.dart` (line ~7) — add `lostConnection` to `AgentServerFailureReason` enum.
- `apps/desktop_flutter/lib/app/core/agents/agent_server_controller.dart` (line ~26) — extend the `errorMessage` switch with a case for `lostConnection`: "The agent server stopped responding. Click Restart to bring it back."

##### Requirements

- Enum gains exactly one value: `lostConnection`.
- `errorMessage` getter returns the new message for `lostConnection`.
- No call site sets the new reason yet — that comes in Issue 3.

##### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `dart analyze` finds no missing switch case.
- [ ] Existing tests still pass.

##### Tests to update

- `apps/desktop_flutter/test/features/agents/agent_server_controller_test.dart` — add a test that `errorMessage` returns the expected text when `_failureReason = lostConnection`.

#### Issue 3 — Wire `HealthPoller` into `AgentServerController`

**Title:** `Poll agent server /health every 15s and transition to failed on loss`
**Milestone:** Phase 1: Foundation
**Depends on:** Issue 1, Issue 2

##### Goal

After the agent server starts successfully, continuously verify it's reachable. If `/health` fails twice in a row, transition `AgentServerController` to `failed` with `lostConnection` reason so the UI can react.

##### Files to touch

- `apps/desktop_flutter/lib/app/core/agents/agent_server_controller.dart`

##### Requirements

- Add `HealthPoller? _poller` field.
- After the existing `if (result.ok)` block in `initialize()`, construct a `HealthPoller(checkFn: () => _service.checkHealth('http://localhost:4001'), onHealthChanged: _onHealthChanged, interval: const Duration(seconds: 15))` and call `_poller!.start()`.
- Implement `_onHealthChanged(bool healthy)`:
  - If `!healthy` and `_status == ready`: set `_status = failed`, `_failureReason = lostConnection`, `notifyListeners()`.
  - If `healthy` and `_status == failed && _failureReason == lostConnection`: set `_status = ready`, `_failureReason = null`, `notifyListeners()`.
- `retry()` must dispose any existing poller before re-calling `initialize()`.
- `dispose()` must dispose the poller in addition to stopping the service.

##### Constraints

- Use `AppConstants.agentLocalBaseUrl` if available; else hardcode `http://localhost:4001`.
- Do not change the existing startup sequence (start → wait for ready → fetch capabilities). Health polling begins after capabilities fetch is dispatched.

##### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Once server is up, `_poller` is non-null and started.
- [ ] When `_service.checkHealth` returns false twice consecutively, status transitions to `failed` with `lostConnection` reason.
- [ ] When health recovers, status transitions back to `ready`.
- [ ] `retry()` cancels the old poller before starting a new lifecycle.
- [ ] No regressions in existing `agent_server_controller_test.dart`.

##### Tests to update

- `apps/desktop_flutter/test/features/agents/agent_server_controller_test.dart` — add tests using a fake `ApiServerService` whose `checkHealth` returns a script of bool values; verify status transitions and that `retry()` cancels and recreates the poller.

---

#### Issue 4 — Expose `connectivityStream` from `AgentsDataSource` with 10s fail timer

**Title:** `Emit WS connectivity stream from AgentsDataSource`
**Milestone:** Phase 1: Foundation
**Depends on:** —

##### Goal

Let `AgentsController` know when the WebSocket has been down long enough to warrant user-visible action, without forcing it to peek at private reconnect state in the data source.

##### Files to touch

- `apps/desktop_flutter/lib/features/agents/data/agents_data_source.dart`
- `apps/desktop_flutter/lib/features/agents/repositories/agents_repository.dart` (add a passthrough getter)
- `apps/desktop_flutter/test/features/agents/agents_data_source_connectivity_test.dart` (new)

##### Requirements

- In `AgentsDataSource`:
  - Add `final StreamController<bool> _connectivityController = StreamController<bool>.broadcast();`.
  - Add `Stream<bool> get connectivityStream => _connectivityController.stream;`.
  - Add `Timer? _disconnectFailTimer`.
  - In `connect()` (after the channel is set up successfully): cancel `_disconnectFailTimer` and emit `true` on the stream.
  - In `_handleDisconnect()`: cancel any prior `_disconnectFailTimer`, then start `Timer(const Duration(seconds: 10), () => _connectivityController.add(false))` so the disconnected signal is delayed by 10s.
  - In `dispose()`: cancel timer; close the controller.
- In `AgentsRepository`: add `Stream<bool> get connectivityStream => _dataSource.connectivityStream;` (also to the interface if one exists).

##### Constraints

- Use a broadcast controller — `AgentsController` will subscribe and we may add additional listeners later.
- Do not change the existing reconnect backoff logic; this work is purely additive.

##### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] After `connect()` succeeds, exactly one `true` event is emitted.
- [ ] On disconnect followed by no reconnect within 10s, exactly one `false` event is emitted.
- [ ] On disconnect followed by successful reconnect within 10s, no `false` event is emitted (timer is cancelled).
- [ ] `dispose()` closes the controller and cancels the timer.

##### Tests to update

- New: `apps/desktop_flutter/test/features/agents/agents_data_source_connectivity_test.dart`. Use `FakeAsync` or a stub WS channel to drive the connect/disconnect/reconnect sequence.

### Phase 2: Controller logic

#### Issue 5 — Add `AgentSessionConnectivity` model

**Title:** `Add AgentSessionConnectivity value type`
**Milestone:** Phase 2: Controller logic
**Depends on:** —

##### Goal

Provide a single typed object the view can read for connectivity-derived UI state.

##### Files to touch

- CREATE `apps/desktop_flutter/lib/features/agents/models/agent_session_connectivity.dart`

##### Requirements

```dart
class AgentSessionConnectivity {
  const AgentSessionConnectivity({
    this.isWsDisconnected = false,
    this.stuckSessionIds = const <String>{},
  });

  final bool isWsDisconnected;
  final Set<String> stuckSessionIds;

  bool isStuck(String sessionId) => stuckSessionIds.contains(sessionId);

  AgentSessionConnectivity copyWith({
    bool? isWsDisconnected,
    Set<String>? stuckSessionIds,
  });
}
```

- Plain value type. No `ChangeNotifier`. No `Equatable`.

##### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Class compiles, `dart format` clean, `flutter analyze` clean.

##### Tests to update

- None required for a pure value type.

#### Issue 6 — Subscribe to `connectivityStream` and expose `connectivity` on `AgentsController`

**Title:** `Subscribe to WS connectivity stream in AgentsController`
**Milestone:** Phase 2: Controller logic
**Depends on:** Issue 4, Issue 5

##### Goal

Translate raw WS connectivity events into the `AgentSessionConnectivity` value exposed to the view.

##### Files to touch

- `apps/desktop_flutter/lib/features/agents/controllers/agents_controller.dart`

##### Requirements

- Add `AgentSessionConnectivity _connectivity = const AgentSessionConnectivity();` field.
- Add getter `AgentSessionConnectivity get connectivity => _connectivity;`.
- Add `StreamSubscription<bool>? _connectivitySub` field.
- In `initialize()` (or the equivalent setup method): subscribe to `_repository.connectivityStream`:
  - On `true`: if `_connectivity.isWsDisconnected`, set `_connectivity = _connectivity.copyWith(isWsDisconnected: false)`, `notifyListeners()`.
  - On `false`: if `!_connectivity.isWsDisconnected`, set `_connectivity = _connectivity.copyWith(isWsDisconnected: true)`, `notifyListeners()`.
- In `dispose()`: cancel `_connectivitySub`.

##### Constraints

- Do not touch session list logic here. Stuck-session tracking is Issue 7.

##### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] When stream emits `false`, `connectivity.isWsDisconnected` becomes `true` and listeners are notified.
- [ ] When stream emits `true`, the flag flips back.
- [ ] `dispose()` cancels the subscription.

##### Tests to update

- `apps/desktop_flutter/test/features/agents/agents_controller_test.dart` — extend `_FakeAgentsRepository` with a `StreamController<bool> _connectivityController` and a `void emitConnectivity(bool v)` helper. Add tests asserting `connectivity.isWsDisconnected` transitions.

#### Issue 7 — Track stuck-Starting sessions in `AgentsController`

**Title:** `Detect sessions stuck in starting state for >30s with no output`
**Milestone:** Phase 2: Controller logic
**Depends on:** Issue 6

##### Goal

Identify sessions where status is `starting`, the live output buffer is empty, and >30s have elapsed since the session was first observed — without changing the session's stored status.

##### Files to touch

- `apps/desktop_flutter/lib/features/agents/controllers/agents_controller.dart`

##### Requirements

- Add `final Map<String, DateTime> _sessionFirstSeenAt = {};` field.
- Add `Timer? _stuckCheckTimer;` field.
- Populate `_sessionFirstSeenAt[id] = DateTime.now()`:
  - On `createSession()` success (after the session is added to `_sessions`).
  - On `_onWsMessage → SessionCreatedMessage`: `_sessionFirstSeenAt[msg.session.id] ??= DateTime.now()`.
  - On `_onWsMessage → SessionsListMessage`: for each newly observed `starting` session, `??= DateTime.now()`.
- Remove from `_sessionFirstSeenAt`:
  - On `_onWsMessage → SessionClosedMessage`.
  - On `_onWsMessage → OutputMessage` if the session is in `starting` (output observed, no longer "stuck").
- In `initialize()`: start `_stuckCheckTimer = Timer.periodic(const Duration(seconds: 5), (_) => _recomputeStuck())`.
- Implement `_recomputeStuck()`:
  - Compute new `Set<String>` of session IDs that satisfy: status is `starting`, `_liveOutputBuffer[id]` is empty, and `DateTime.now().difference(_sessionFirstSeenAt[id]!) > Duration(seconds: 30)`.
  - If the set differs from `_connectivity.stuckSessionIds`, update via `copyWith` and `notifyListeners()`.
- In `dispose()`: cancel `_stuckCheckTimer`.

##### Constraints

- Do NOT mutate `AgentSession.status` on the model. Stuck is a view-layer hint, not a stored state.
- Keep periodic check cheap (set comparison; no allocations in steady state).

##### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A starting session with no output for >30s appears in `connectivity.stuckSessionIds`.
- [ ] When output arrives, the session is removed from the set on the next tick (or immediately if also cleared in `OutputMessage` handler).
- [ ] When the session is closed, it is removed from `_sessionFirstSeenAt` and from `stuckSessionIds`.

##### Tests to update

- `agents_controller_test.dart` — using `FakeAsync` or by exposing an `@visibleForTesting` setter on `_sessionFirstSeenAt`, assert detection at 30s, clearance on output, clearance on close.

---

#### Issue 8 — Add `reconnectSession(String id)` to `AgentsController`

**Title:** `Implement single-button reconnect with server-status branching`
**Milestone:** Phase 2: Controller logic
**Depends on:** Issue 6

##### Goal

A single button on a stuck session that does the right thing whether the local server is down or just the session is stale.

##### Files to touch

- `apps/desktop_flutter/lib/features/agents/controllers/agents_controller.dart`

##### Requirements

- Add `bool _reconnecting = false` guard.
- New method:

  ```dart
  Future<void> reconnectSession(String id) async {
    if (_reconnecting) return;
    _reconnecting = true;
    try {
      if (!_agentServerController.isReady) {
        await _agentServerController.retry();
        await load();
        return;
      }
      _repository.send({'type': 'session.subscribe', 'id': id});
      final result = await _repository.getSession(id);
      if (_selectedSessionId == id) {
        _transcript = result.messages;
        notifyListeners();
      }
    } catch (e) {
      _error = e.toString();
      notifyListeners();
    } finally {
      _reconnecting = false;
    }
  }
  ```

##### Constraints

- The reconnect-in-progress guard prevents double-clicks from triggering two concurrent retries.
- Do not introduce a separate "reconnect server only" path — the single method handles both cases.

##### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Calling `reconnectSession` while server is not ready calls `agentServerController.retry()` then `load()`.
- [ ] Calling `reconnectSession` while server is ready issues a `session.subscribe` and refreshes the transcript.
- [ ] Concurrent calls are coalesced via `_reconnecting`.

##### Tests to update

- `agents_controller_test.dart` — extend `_FakeAgentServerController` with a `retryCallCount` to verify both branches.

---

#### Issue 9 — Offline `closeSession` fallback in `AgentsController`

**Title:** `Force-close stale sessions client-side when server is unreachable`
**Milestone:** Phase 2: Controller logic
**Depends on:** —

##### Goal

When the local agent server is down, the existing Close button quietly fails (DELETE returns connection refused) and the stale session is stuck in the UI. Fall back to removing the session from the in-memory list when the server isn't reachable.

##### Files to touch

- `apps/desktop_flutter/lib/features/agents/controllers/agents_controller.dart`

##### Requirements

- At the top of `closeSession(String id)`:

  ```dart
  if (!_agentServerController.isReady) {
    _sessions = _sessions.where((s) => s.id != id).toList();
    if (_selectedSessionId == id) _selectedSessionId = null;
    _liveOutputBuffer.remove(id);
    _sessionFirstSeenAt.remove(id);
    notifyListeners();
    return;
  }
  ```

- The existing online path remains unchanged.

##### Constraints

- The DB row may or may not already be `closed` — that's fine. The next successful `sessions.list` after the server comes back will reconcile.

##### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] When `agentServerController.status != ready`, calling `closeSession` removes the session from `_sessions` synchronously and clears related maps.
- [ ] When `agentServerController.status == ready`, the existing DELETE path is taken unchanged.

##### Tests to update

- `agents_controller_test.dart` — assert both paths behave correctly. Reuse the existing `_FakeAgentServerController` with a settable `isReady`.

#### Issue 10 — Status dot for agent server in `_SessionListHeader`

**Title:** `Show agent server health dot next to Manage agents`
**Milestone:** Phase 3: UI

##### Goal

At-a-glance visibility of agent-server health from the Agents screen.

##### Files to touch

- `apps/desktop_flutter/lib/features/agents/views/agents_view.dart`

##### Requirements

- Define a new private `_AgentServerStatusDot` `StatelessWidget` at the bottom of the file.
  - Reads `context.watch<AgentServerController>().status`.
  - Renders an 8×8 circle: `rhythm.success` (green) when `ready`, `rhythm.warning` (amber) when `starting`, `rhythm.danger` (red) when `failed`.
  - Wraps in `Tooltip` with the status text.
- In `_SessionListHeader.build`, append the dot to the Row containing the "Manage agents" `TextButton.icon` (around line 460), separated by `SizedBox(width: 8)`.

##### Constraints

- Keep the widget private; do not extract to a separate file (matches existing `_StatusDot` convention).
- Do not change the existing header layout other than appending the dot.

##### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Dot appears next to "Manage agents".
- [ ] Color reflects status via the rhythm theme tokens.
- [ ] Tooltip is present on hover.

##### Tests to update

- None (pure widget rendering — covered by manual smoke test).

#### Issue 11 — Inline `_DisconnectedBanner` in `_SessionListPanel`

**Title:** `Show inline banner with Restart button when agent server is down`
**Milestone:** Phase 3: UI
**Depends on:** Issue 3, Issue 6

##### Goal

When `AgentServerController.status != ready` or `controller.connectivity.isWsDisconnected`, show a banner inside the Agents view with a "Restart agent server" button.

##### Files to touch

- `apps/desktop_flutter/lib/features/agents/views/agents_view.dart`

##### Requirements

- New private `_DisconnectedBanner` `StatelessWidget` at the bottom of the file.
  - Reads `AgentServerController` and `AgentsController` via `context.watch`.
  - Returns `const SizedBox.shrink()` when `agentServerController.status == ready && !controller.connectivity.isWsDisconnected`.
  - Otherwise: a `Container` with `rhythm.danger`-tinted background, horizontal padding, 1px border, containing an icon + message + `TextButton('Restart agent server', onPressed: () => context.read<AgentServerController>().retry())`.
  - Message text: if `status != ready`, use `agentServerController.errorMessage` (or "Agent server unavailable" if null). If only `isWsDisconnected`, use "Connection lost — reconnecting…".
- Insert `const _DisconnectedBanner()` inside `_SessionListPanel`, after the existing `Divider` and before the `Expanded` session list.

##### Constraints

- Banner consumes vertical space only when visible.
- Do not block list interactions when banner is shown.

##### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Banner appears when status is `starting`, `failed`, or when WS is disconnected for >10s.
- [ ] "Restart agent server" calls `AgentServerController.retry()`.
- [ ] Banner disappears once status returns to `ready` and connectivity is restored.

##### Tests to update

- None (pure widget rendering — covered by manual smoke test).

#### Issue 12 — Reconnect button on `_TranscriptHeader`

**Title:** `Add Reconnect button to transcript header for stuck sessions`
**Milestone:** Phase 3: UI
**Depends on:** Issue 8, Issue 6

##### Goal

Give the user a one-click way to recover from a stuck `Starting` session or a disconnected WS without manually closing the session.

##### Files to touch

- `apps/desktop_flutter/lib/features/agents/views/agents_view.dart` (lines 878–926 — `_TranscriptHeader`)

##### Requirements

- Add `final agentServerController = context.watch<AgentServerController>();` next to the existing controller read.
- Compute `final showReconnect = agentServerController.status != AgentServerStatus.ready || controller.connectivity.isWsDisconnected;`.
- Insert immediately before the existing close `IconButton`:

  ```dart
  if (showReconnect) ...[
    OutlinedButton(
      onPressed: () => context.read<AgentsController>().reconnectSession(session.id),
      style: OutlinedButton.styleFrom(
        foregroundColor: context.rhythm.accent,
        side: BorderSide(color: context.rhythm.border),
        padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 10, vertical: 6),
        minimumSize: Size.zero,
        tapTargetSize: MaterialTapTargetSize.shrinkWrap,
        shape: RoundedRectangleBorder(borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(RhythmRadius.md)),
      ),
      child: const Text('Reconnect', style: TextStyle(fontSize: 12, fontWeight: FontWeight.w600)),
    ),
    const SizedBox(width: 6),
  ],
  ```
- Update the close `IconButton`'s `tooltip` to "Force close" when `!agentServerController.isReady`, else "Close session".

##### Constraints

- Reconnect button only renders when needed.
- Close button remains, falling through to the offline path from Issue 9 when server is unreachable.

##### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Reconnect appears when server is down or WS disconnected.
- [ ] Reconnect calls `AgentsController.reconnectSession(session.id)`.
- [ ] Close button tooltip changes contextually.

##### Tests to update

- None (covered by smoke test).

#### Issue 13 — Stuck-Starting hint in `_SessionRow`

**Title:** `Show "No output yet" hint on stuck sessions`
**Milestone:** Phase 3: UI
**Depends on:** Issue 7

##### Goal

Surface the stuck-Starting computed state inline in the session row.

##### Files to touch

- `apps/desktop_flutter/lib/features/agents/views/agents_view.dart`

##### Requirements

- Extend `_SessionRow` with `required this.isStuck` (bool) constructor parameter.
- In `_SessionListPanel` (the parent that constructs `_SessionRow`), pass `isStuck: controller.connectivity.isStuck(session.id)`. This keeps `_SessionRow` a `StatelessWidget` and mirrors the existing `isWorking` parameter pattern.
- In `_SessionRow.build`, after the `lastPreview` text (or equivalent secondary line), conditionally render:

  ```dart
  if (isStuck)
    Padding(
      padding: const EdgeInsets.only(top: 4),
      child: Text(
        'No output yet — the agent may be stuck',
        style: TextStyle(fontSize: 10, color: context.rhythm.warning, fontStyle: FontStyle.italic),
      ),
    ),
  ```

##### Constraints

- Do not convert `_SessionRow` to a `StatefulWidget`.
- Do not change session status; this is purely view-layer.

##### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Hint appears on rows where `connectivity.isStuck(id)` is true.
- [ ] Hint disappears when output arrives or session is closed.

##### Tests to update

- None (covered by smoke test; logic is covered by Issue 7's controller tests).

#### Issue 14 — Dynamic agent list in `_NewSessionDialog`

**Title:** `Render New Session agent toggles from AgentConfigsController.enabledAgents`
**Milestone:** Phase 3: UI
**Depends on:** —

##### Goal

Replace the hardcoded `claude-code` / `codex` branches with a loop over enabled agent configs, showing disabled "(not installed)" buttons for configs whose CLI isn't on the system.

##### Files to touch

- `apps/desktop_flutter/lib/features/agents/views/agents_view.dart`
  - `_showNewSessionDialog` wrapper (~line 344) — add `AgentConfigsController` provider.
  - `_NewSessionDialog` build (lines 1480–1580) — replace branches.
  - `_AgentToggleButton` (lines 1721–1757) — add `enabled` and `disabledLabel` params.
  - Add `_colorForAgent(String id)` helper.

##### Requirements

1. **Provider wiring.** In `_showNewSessionDialog`, add `ChangeNotifierProvider.value(value: context.read<AgentConfigsController>(), child: ...)` so the dialog's `context.watch<AgentConfigsController>()` works.
2. **Loop replacement.** In `_NewSessionDialog.build`, replace the hardcoded `claudeAvailable` / `codexAvailable` block (lines 1480–1490, 1541–1580) with:

   ```dart
   final agentConfigs = context.watch<AgentConfigsController>();
   final agentServerController = context.watch<AgentServerController>();
   final enabledAgents = agentConfigs.enabledAgents;

   // ...
   if (enabledAgents.isNotEmpty) ...[
     Text('Agent', /* existing label style */),
     const SizedBox(height: 6),
     Container(
       decoration: /* existing decoration */,
       child: Row(
         children: [
           for (final config in enabledAgents)
             Expanded(
               child: _AgentToggleButton(
                 label: config.label,
                 selected: _agentId == config.id,
                 color: _colorForAgent(config.id),
                 enabled: agentServerController.isAgentAvailable(config.id),
                 disabledLabel: '(not installed)',
                 onTap: agentServerController.isAgentAvailable(config.id)
                     ? () => setState(() => _agentId = config.id)
                     : null,
               ),
             ),
         ],
       ),
     ),
     const SizedBox(height: 14),
   ],
   ```

3. **Default agent.** Replace the existing `_agentId = 'claude-code'` initializer with a computed default in `initState` (or first build): the first `enabledAgents` entry where `agentServerController.isAgentAvailable(id) == true`. If none are installed, fall back to `enabledAgents.first.id` (button will render disabled).
4. **`_AgentToggleButton` signature.** Add `final bool enabled` (default `true`) and `final String? disabledLabel`. When `!enabled`, wrap the rendered button in `IgnorePointer` (or set `onTap: null` directly), render label muted via theme, and append `disabledLabel` as a smaller muted line.
5. **`_colorForAgent` helper.**
   ```dart
   Color _colorForAgent(String id) => switch (id) {
     'claude-code' => const Color(0xFF6B46C1),
     'codex' => const Color(0xFF059669),
     _ => context.rhythm.accent,
   };
   ```

##### Constraints

- All three call paths into the dialog (button, keyboard shortcut, task-driven open) must still work — the provider wiring is in the shared wrapper.
- Don't remove the `claude-code` / `codex` color constants — fold them into the helper.

##### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Dialog renders one toggle per enabled config.
- [ ] Configs whose CLI is not installed render disabled with "(not installed)" appended.
- [ ] Default selection is the first installed enabled config; if none installed, falls back to the first enabled config (still disabled).
- [ ] Selecting a toggle persists into the create-session payload.
- [ ] No regression for the existing claude-code/codex flow when both are installed.

##### Tests to update

- None required for UI; if a widget test for `_NewSessionDialog` exists, update it to drive `enabledAgents` instead of hardcoded checks.

---

### Phase 4: Manual finalization

#### Step 15 — Rebuild + reinstall app, verify smoke checks

**Not a GitHub issue — a manual step performed once Phase 3 is merged.**

1. Trigger the `desktop_release.yml` workflow with a fresh patch version tag.
2. Download the resulting DMG, install into `/Applications`.
3. Verify:
   - `rg -n "agentConfigs|agent_configs|/agent-configs" /Applications/Rhythm.app/Contents/Resources/api_server/dist` returns matches.
   - `sqlite3 "$HOME/Library/Application Support/Rhythm/rhythm.db" ".tables"` includes `agent_configs`.
   - `curl -sS http://localhost:4001/health` returns 200 once the app is running.
   - Open Agents screen, observe the green status dot.
   - Click "Manage agents" — `ManageAgentsView` opens.
   - Click `+ New` — New Session dialog shows toggle per enabled config.
   - Force-stop the bundled Node process from Activity Monitor; observe banner + Reconnect appearing; click Restart agent server; observe recovery.
